reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. `[Fintype G]` with `card G = 105` (using `open Fintype` so `card` resolves to `Fintype.card`) correctly encodes "group of order 105". `P : Sylow 3 G` with `[P.Normal]` corresponds to "a Sylow 3-subgroup is normal" (and since all Sylow p-subgroups are conjugate, "a normal Sylow 3-subgroup exists" is equivalent to "every Sylow 3-subgroup is normal", which matches the universal quantification over P in the Lean signature). The conclusion `∀ a b : G, a*b = b*a` is precisely the standard definition of G being abelian. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and no quantifier structure is altered.
